subject: TOOL-TELEMETRY implementation source-level validation

conditions.description: serviceOption pattern (not catchAll) for service availability

evidence.detail: Effect.serviceOption(AppFileSystem.Service) is used with Option.match({ onNone: logWarning, onSome: writeRecord }). catchAll is only used for the I/O write failure (lines 304, 353), not for service availability.

Three distinct logWarning sites: (a) AppFileSystem unavailable (lines 277, 324), (b) telemetry write failed (lines 305, 353), (c) UnknownError detected (line 364). All failures are logged, not silently swallowed.

InstanceState.context is yielded inside the tap success callback (line 273) and tapError callback (line 320). These callbacks execute at tool invocation time, not at tool definition time. The context resolves the session directory dynamically.

errorTag chain: (error as any)?._tag ?? (error as any)?.constructor?.name ?? 'unknown' (line 318). An explicit logWarning is emitted when errorTag === 'unknown' || errorTag === 'UnknownError' (line 362-370).

conditions.description: Concurrent test written (8 invocations, distinct callIDs)

evidence.detail: The test file exists but contains only 1 test (a smoke test: 'basic tool execution works'). Required: 6+ tests including a concurrent test with 8 invocations and distinct callIDs. Missing tests: concurrent invocation telemetry, error taxonomy logging, UnknownError detection, telemetry file I/O verification, AppFileSystem unavailable fallback, and write-failure resilience.

conditions.description: Typecheck passes — define() return type preserved

evidence.detail: The define() function signature is unchanged: Effect.Effect<Info<Parameters, Result>, never, R | Truncate.Service | Agent.Service> & { id: ID }. No type errors originate from tool.ts or analytics.ts. However, the project has 254+ pre-existing type errors in unrelated files (campaign/, plugin/, test/). The pipeline uses as any casts (lines 236, 269, 366, 384) that suppress type safety within the compose chain.

additional_validations.description: Telemetry pipeline placement: AFTER retry/timeout/semaphore, BEFORE orDie

evidence.detail: Layer 1 (core, line 186) → Layer 2 (retry, line 239) → Layer 3 (timeout, line 246) → Layer 4 (semaphore, line 263) → Layer 5a (telemetry tap, line 268) → Layer 5b (tapDefect + orDie, line 373). Telemetry observes the final outcome after all retry, timeout, and semaphore gates, before orDie finalization.

The 5 tagged errors (InvalidArgumentsError _tag='ToolInvalidArgumentsError', ToolError _tag='ToolError', TimeoutError _tag='TimeoutError', TransientError _tag='TransientError', ValidationError _tag='ValidationError') are captured via their _tag property. Unknown errors fall through to constructor.name → 'unknown'. The UnknownError logWarning fires for untagged/unidentified errors.

additional_validations.description: analytics.ts tool_invocations metric follows JSONL query pattern

evidence.detail: The tool_invocations metric (lines 101-108) follows the exact same pattern as bash_usage, smart_tool_usage, heartbeat, and feedback: read JSONL file → split by newline → count lines. Consistent with existing codebase conventions.

additional_validations.description: Telemetry I/O failure never breaks tool execution

evidence.detail: Both the success and error telemetry write paths use Effect.catchAll((err) => Effect.logWarning(...)) (lines 304, 353). If writing the telemetry record fails, the error is logged and the tool execution continues unaffected. The catchAll is inside the tap callback, so the tap never fails the pipeline.

additional_validations.description: No unintended changes in git diff

evidence.detail: The tool-telemetry implementation changes are limited to: tool.ts (telemetry tap/tapError/tapDefect, imports for AppFileSystem/InstanceState/path/CacheKey), analytics.ts (tool_invocations metric), cache.ts (span annotation enrichment). Other diff files (registry.ts, read.ts, typed-result.ts, session/tools.ts, event/event-names.ts, .opencode/tools/*) belong to other lanes and are not part of this telemetry slice. Total diff: 33 files, 2246 lines.
define() function signature unchanged. The telemetry changes are contained within the wrap() function's inner execute closure. The exported type contract (Def<P, M>, Info<P, M>) remains intact.

defects.description: Test file incomplete — only 1 of 6+ required tests written
defects.detail: The test file exists (49 lines, 1 smoke test) but is drastically incomplete. Missing: concurrent invocation test (8 invocations with distinct callIDs), error taxonomy verification (all 5 tagged errors), UnknownError detection test, telemetry file I/O verification, AppFileSystem fallback test, and write-failure resilience test.

defects.description: InstanceState dependency in telemetry tap could defect if not provided
defects.detail: The telemetry tap yields InstanceState.context without serviceOption wrapping. If InstanceState is missing from the environment at invocation time, the tap defects and cascades through orDie. AppFileSystem uses serviceOption (safe pattern) but InstanceState does not. In practice, InstanceState is always provided in the tool environment, so low risk.

defects.description: Pipeline uses as any casts that suppress type safety
defects.detail: The pipeline composition relies on as any casts (lines 236, 269-366, 384) to work around Effect's type constraints in the dynamic layer composition. This is a pragmatic pattern used elsewhere in the codebase but means the type system cannot verify correctness of the pipeline chain.

pre_existing_issues_noted.detail: Type comparison errors with EventName enum — pre-existing
Redeclared export and type assignability errors — pre-existing
Type errors with 21 arguments and missing pipe property — pre-existing (cache pipeline bug)
Missing exported properties (makeScopedShell, makeScopedFetch, etc.) — pre-existing

admission_detail: The implementation logic (telemetry tap/tapError placement, error taxonomy, analytics integration, I/O failure isolation) passes all functional and structural checks. However, the test file is 80% incomplete. Required tests for concurrent execution (8 invocations, distinct callIDs), error taxonomy coverage, and resilience scenarios (AppFileSystem unavailable, write failure) are missing. The test file must be completed before this slice can be admitted for review.
repair_required: Add concurrent invocation test: 8 parallel tool calls with unique callIDs, verify all records written to tool_invocations.v1.jsonl
Add error taxonomy test: exercise each of the 5 tagged errors, verify correct error_tag in JSONL record
Add UnknownError detection test: throw a plain Error, verify logWarning fired and error_tag is 'Error' (constructor.name) or 'unknown'
Add AppFileSystem unavailable test: run tool without AppFileSystem layer, verify logWarning fired
Add telemetry write failure resilience test: mock/break the filesystem write, verify tool execution still succeeds
Add success telemetry test: verify correct JSONL record structure (timestamp, tool name, session_id, message_id, elapsed_ms, status, call_id)
